name: H.R. Giger Museum
description: The bizarre visions of H.R. Giger, tucked away in a Swiss medieval city.

name: Berggasthaus Aescher Wildkirchli
description: This 170-year-old Swiss guesthouse built into the side of a mountain is best accessed by a cable car.

name: Saut de Brot
description: This lovely little bridge hidden in a Switzerland gorge is essentially a fairytale.

name: Hammetschwand Elevator
description: The tallest outdoor elevator in Europe rockets passengers on a dizzyingly precarious ride to the top of a mountain.

name: St. Beatus Cave
description: Caves famous for the dragon-slaying saint who lived there.

name: Trift Bridge
description: This amazing pedestrian suspension bridge in the Swiss Alps would give Indiana Jones nightmares.

name: The Child Eater of Bern
description: A nearly 500-year-old sculpture depicts a man eating a sack of babies, and no one is sure why.

name: Höllgrotten Caves
description: These spooky and unusual stone formations create an underworldly landscape.

name: Abbey Library of Saint Gall
description: This is not only one of the oldest collections in Europe, but also possibly the most beautiful.

name: Lion of Lucerne
description: This memorial commemorating fallen Swiss Guards of the French Revolution has been called the saddest stone in the world.

name: Sphinx Observatory
description: A historic observatory precariously perched at the "Top of Europe."

name: CERN
description: Home of the Large Hadron Collider and the birthplace of the World Wide Web.

name: Stoosbahn Funicular
description: The steepest classic funicular in the world glides along a dizzying incline.

name: The Reichenbach Falls
description: The alpine falls where Sherlock Holmes apparently met his demise can actually be visited.

name: Hotel Belvédère
description: A once-thriving hotel in the Swiss Alps couldn't compete with climate change.

name: Dance of Death Bridge
description: This historic covered bridge contains a series of macabre paintings that communicate one thing: everyone dies.

name: Teufelsbrucke
description: A breathtaking bridge that crosses a vast gorge over a river's torrents, and has a tale of a devil architect attached.

name: Cabaret Voltaire: the Dada House
description: One hundred years ago this small house in downtown Zürich became the birthplace of the Dada movement.

name: Oepfelchammer
description: A 200-year-old tavern challenges guests to finish a glass of wine while hanging from a ceiling beam.

name: Schilthorn Piz Gloria
description: A cablecar-station-turned-revolving restaurant with 360-degree views of the Swiss skyline.

name: The Niesenbahn
description: Traversing steep Alpine slopes, this engineering marvel is one of the longest and highest funiculars in the world.

name: Leuk Charnel House
description: For centuries no one knew there were hundreds of bones and precious artwork hidden beneath this Swiss church.

name: Barryland
description: Enormous, fluffy dogs frolic around this museum dedicated to a legendary Saint Bernard.

name: Evionnaz Adventure Labyrinth
description: World's largest permanent maze made of 18,000 conifers.

name: MFO Park
description: Vines grow up the metal bars in this public park created from an old machine factory.

name: Moulagenmuseum
description: Museum dedicated to wax representations of disfiguring diseases.

name: Sukkulenten-Sammlung Zürich
description: See the rare bloom of the "Queen of the Night" cactus at one of the world's largest succulent collections.

name: Grottes de Vallorbe
description: Over 3km of caverns and caves at the Switzerland-France border contain the largest underground river in Central Europe.

name: Haus Hiltl
description: Featuring a meatless buffet fit for kings, this family-owned Zurich eatery is considered the oldest vegetarian restaurant in the West.

name: United Nations Ceiling
description: This colorful but controversial ceiling has been likened to a 21st-century Sistine Chapel.

name: Lavaux
description: Swiss vineyards in a UNESCO heritage site.

name: Bruno Weber's Weinrebenpark
description: A mythically themed self-built sculpture park.

name: Kaltbach Cave
description: A 22-million-year-old sandstone cave is a natural habitat for award-winning cheese.

name: Frog Museum
description: 150-year-old satirical tableaus made from stuffed frogs.

name: Einsteinhaus
description: This simple apartment may have inspired some of the most important theories in the history of science.

name: Lac Souterrain de Saint-Léonard
description: The largest underground lake in Europe.

name: Schrödinger's Cat
description: Hidden in the garden of physicist Erwin Schrödinger's house is a life-size figure of a cat from his famous thought experiment.

name: Swiss Museum of Games
description: A Swiss board game museum housed inside a picture-perfect castle.

name: Heureka Useless Machine
description: One of a series of no-purpose kinetic artworks by Swiss sculptor Jean Tinguely.

name: Hexenmuseum Schweiz
description: The excellent Swiss museum of witchcraft is led by a modern witch.

name: Bärengraben
description: The bears of Bern have been a city highlight for over 500 years.

name: Méret Oppenheim Fountain
description: The Swiss artist's bulbous and overgrown shape-shifting sculpture controversially graces the center of Bern.

name: La Fourchette (The Fork)
description: At 929 pounds and 26 feet tall, this stainless steel fork can only be used by the likes of King Kong.

name: St. Peter’s Island
description: The idyllic island where the philosopher Jean-Jacques Rousseau spent “the happiest time of his life.”

name: Kulturama
description: Zurich's evolution museum hosts a unique collection of skeletons among its natural history treasures.

name: Collection de l'Art Brut
description: Artworks by loners, prisoners and the criminally insane.

name: Villa Patumbah
description: This eclectic architectural gem played witness to Zurich’s colonial past.

name: Bhutanese Bridge Over the Illgraben
description: A symbol of friendship between the mountainous regions of Switzerland and Bhutan.

name: Villa Patumbah
description: This eclectic architectural gem played witness to Zurich’s colonial past.

name: Psychiatrie-Museum Bern
description: This small museum displays a collection of outsider art and scale models of past "treatments" for mental illness.

name: Villa Diodati
description: Where Mary Shelley’s 'Frankenstein' and John William Polidori's 'The Vampyre' were born.

name: La Tulipe
description: A rare, flower-shaped example of Brutalist architecture in Switzerland.

name: Maison d'Ailleurs
description: Museum of science fiction, utopias, and extraordinary journeys of the mind.

name: The Bisses of Valais
description: Medieval irrigation channels built into the sides of mountains provided Valais with water for hundreds of years.

name: Schloss Habsburg (Habsburg Castle)
description: An 11th-century fortress home to one of the most influential royal houses in medieval Europe.

name: Jungfrau Park
description: A Swiss amusement park entirely dedicated to the theory of ancient astronauts.

name: Anna Göldi Museum
description: The last woman executed for witchcraft in Europe is remembered in this Swiss museum.

name: Palais de Rumine
description: Florentine Renaissance style building in Switzerland, home to the world's largest taxidermy Great White Shark.

name: Museum of Communication
description: Video games, body language, and postal history are all on display in this Swiss museum.

name: Freddie Mercury Statue
description: This statue pays tribute to the iconic musician in the place where he recorded his final studio album.

name: The Births of the Full Moon
description: A constellation of golden cobblestones shows the names of local children born under a full moon.

name: Audrey Hepburn’s Grave
description: The immortal Hollywood star rests in peace in an unassuming Swiss village cemetery.

name: Pavillion Le Corbusier
description: Constructed along the shores of Lake Zurich, this pavilion is a walk-through work of art.

name: The Henkermuseum
description: Extensive private collection of authentic Medieval devices used for human torture and execution.

name: Abbey St. Maurice d'Agaune
description: Abbey built on the ruins of Roman catacombs.

name: Birthplace of the Web
description: The World Wide Web was created in this nondescript corridor at CERN.

name: Treille Promenade
description: One of the longest wooden benches in the world snakes around Geneva's Treille Promenade.

name: Sauriermuseum Aathal
description: Europe's largest dinosaur museum was founded by a passionate autodidact.

name: Bambibrunnen (Bambi Fountain)
description: This charming fountain honors Felix Salten, the creator of the iconic storybook deer.

name: Villa Rose
description: This secret Swiss fortress is disguised as a harmless pink house, complete with fake windows.

name: Gravestone of Jorge Luis Borges
description: This famous writer's tombstone is a literary pilgrimage site that was publicly defiled by a rival.

name: Statue of Frankenstein's Monster
description: One of the most historical literary characters continues to stroll his hometown.

name: Jaquet-Droz Automata
description: These magnificent 240-year-old machines are considered distant ancestors of the computer.

name: Pharmazie-Historisches Museum der Universität Basel
description: One of the world's largest collections of pharmaceutical artifacts.

name: Taxidermy Whale of Fribourg
description: This unique preservation is one of the only taxidermies in the world made with real whale skin.

name: Solvay Hut
description: One of the highest emergency huts in the world has been waiting almost a century to give imperiled climbers a place to lie down.

name: Hoosesagg Museum (Pants Pocket Museum)
description: The smallest museum in Basel, Switzerland, is a two-foot-by-two-foot window in the door of a 600-year-old house.

name: Barabas Hotel Luzern
description: Lucerne's central prison was converted into a tourist hotel with a bare minimum of changes.

name: Toblerone Line
description: This long line of cement defensive wedges is named after the famous chocolate bar for obvious reasons.

name: Dreamfactory's World of Wonders
description: A Swiss magician's vault of oddities.

name: The Bodmer Library
description: A trove of ridiculously valuable prints and manuscripts under one roof.

name: Naturhistorisches Museum
description: Natural history museum displaying a famous taxidermied St. Bernard.

name: Greisinger Museum
description: Middle-earth in the middle of Switzerland.

name: Synergy Village
description: A communal oasis on Lake Zurich.

name: Naters Beinhaus
description: This ossuary is more than five centuries old and is heralded as one of the finest in Switzerland.

name: 'Seesicht' ('Lake View')
description: Walk down these stairs to get an underwater view of Switzerland's Lake Zug.

name: Museum Tinguely
description: An interactive exhibition space dedicated to the kinetic sculptures of Swiss artist Jean Tinguely.

name: Lutry Menhirs
description: Standing stones found on a construction site are re-erected.

name: Hotel Arbez
description: Part of this hotel resides in Switzerland, the other in France, and the border splits the honeymoon suite.

name: Museum ENTER
description: Its vast collection of wondrous gadgets details the fascinating history of information technology.

name: Spielzeug Welten Museum
description: An unassuming Swiss institution houses Europe's largest toy museum.

name: Pegasus Small World Toy Museum
description: Museum featuring strange and enchanting toys of the past.

name: Urania Observatory
description: Switzerland's oldest public observatory is an important part of the Zurich cityscape.

name: Chaiselongue
description: This colossal couch is an unlikely monument to Europe's first female Doctor of Law.

name: The World's Largest Chocolate Fountain
description: A 30-foot-high stream of chocolate flows continuously at the Lindt Home of Chocolate.

name: Ponte dei Salti
description: This medieval footbridge in the Swiss Alps is known to attract cliff-jumping daredevils.

name: Gotthard Base Tunnel
description: The world's longest and deepest train tunnel.

name: Nataraj Statue (The CERN Shiva)
description: This statue of a Hindu cosmic dancer at CERN is a metaphor for modern physics.

name: Brusio Spiral Viaduct
description: This work of engineering is imbued with elegance and ingeniousness.

name: Mountain Studios
description: A permanent exhibition honors Queen, which recorded seven albums at the casino-based studio, including “Under Pressure” with David Bowie.

name: Bahnhof Eismeer (Eismeer Railroad Station)
description: The second-highest train station in Europe.

name: Findling vom Geissturm
description: This massive boulder was hurled into a Zurich church when a gunpowder depot was struck by lightning in 1652.

name: Hotel Everland
description: Explore the subjective dream of a hotel.

name: Museum Stemmler
description: This taxidermilogical collection was assembled by a peltmonger turned animal activist.

name: Monte Verità
description: The Swiss hilltop housed an early 20th-century vegetarian nudist colony.

name: L’horloge Fleurie (The Flower Clock)
description: The world's largest second-hand graces a clock surrounded by constantly changing flower beds.

name: Ehgraben (Medieval Sewage Ditch)
description: Use a key to unlock this medieval sewer tunnel.

name: Anatomical Museum Basel
description: A journey through every aspect of the human body.

name: Musée d'Horlogerie du Locle
description: An amazing clockwork museum in the Val de Travers holds examples of various prestigious legacies horological history.

name: 'Eine gefahrliche Uberfahrt' ('A Dangerous Crossing')
description: Along the Rhine, about a mile from the historic Munster, learn the heartrending story of two lovers torn asunder, cast into unknown layers of time and space.

name: The Schwamendingen X
description: Unconventional site where the tracks of Zurich tramcars intersect.

name: EPFL Learning Center
description: The university library shaped like a giant piece of Swiss cheese.

name: Musée d'Histoire des Sciences (Museum of History of Science)
description: A treasure trove of scientific instruments from the 17th and 18th centuries.

name: Brunswick Monument
description: An opulent monument built for a duke who donated his entire fortune to the city of Geneva.

name: Trümmelbach Falls
description: Visit Europe's largest subterranean waterfall, carved by water from the glaciers of the towering peaks surrounding the valley.

name: Spalentor
description: The most impressive of Basel's three remaining city gates.

name: Null Stern Hotel Museum
description: Created by a pair of artists, this former hotel was designed to provide a minimum of everything including comfort.

name: Klingende Sammlung
description: An amazing collection of rare wind instruments where visitors can give the iconic Swiss Alphorn a toot.

name: Museum im Lagerhaus
description: A unique collection of Swiss art brut and naïve art displayed in an old warehouse.

name: Lindenhof Keller
description: Underneath the well-known Lindenhof hides a secret cellar documenting Zurich's early history.

name: Les Pleureuses
description: What this Good Friday parade lacks in baroque filigree it makes up in somber gothic imagery.

name: 'Wandering the Immeasurable'
description: CERN's beautiful, bending sculpture is a tribute to the spread of knowledge.

name: Tinguely Fountain
description: This artistic fountain display pays homage to this area's theatrical history.

name: Grave of Rainer Maria Rilke
description: The gravestone of a famous poet features a fittingly poetic epitaph chosen by the writer himself.

name: Pointe de la Jonction
description: Where two rivers meet—but don’t mix.

name: Dreiländereck
description: A futuristic rocket marks the spot where three borders meet.

name: 'Paxmal'
description: A monument to peace, built by one man over the course of 25 years.

name: Verzasca Dam
description: If James Bond bungee-jumped off the side of a 720-foot dam, would you do it too?

name: 'Echodrome'
description: This large sculpture of white plates and forms is actually a huge sound amplifier.

name: Rütli Meadow
description: This scenic spot is known as the birthplace of Switzerland.

name: JURASSICA Museum
description: This little museum is packed with information about the region's past and present biodiversity.

name: Johanna Spyri Museum
description: Its collection commemorates the author of the iconic children's book "Heidi."

name: The King of Olten
description: A bronze statue commemorating his royal meowjesty, Toulouse.

name: Kornhauskeller Restaurant
description: Dining in this grain cellar feels like stepping onto a fantasy film set.

name: Aussichtsturm Hardwald
description: A massive observation tower that features gorgeous views and unique architecture.

name: Iron Library
description: Everything from alchemy books to building plans for the Eiffel Tower are housed inside this museum.

name: Pyramides d'Euseigne
description: These spindly, stone-capped formations are the product of glacial deposits and thousands of years of erosion.

name: Straw Museum
description: A rare museum dedicated to a rather overlooked material in the history of fashion.

name: Polenmuseum Rapperswil (Polish Museum Rapperswil)
description: A 13th-century Swiss castle houses a collection of artifacts from the Polish diaspora.

name: Mère Royaume
description: On the side of a building, a depiction of the woman who became a local legend by tossing soup out her window.

name: John Calvin's Chair
description: A plain wooden seat that once belonged to one of the most prominent figures of the Protestant Reformation.

name: Tour des Sorciers
description: Visit a prison tower used during witch tribunals.

name: Mühlerama Museum
description: Grind your own flour to bake your own bread at a 100-year-old mill.

name: Gigerplatz Fountain
description: Study the Alien etchings in this fountain while you refill your water bottle.

name: Giacometti Halle
description: Ornate lobby of a police station is decorated with colorful murals by Augusto Giacometti.

name: Münster Reaction Ferry
description: This ferry carries passengers across the Rhine propelled by no engine or paddles but by the river's current.

name: Church of St. Luzi Crypt
description: The remains of St. Lucius of Chur were said to be kept in this crypt for several centuries.

name: Mountaineers' Cemetery
description: This Swiss cemetery is reserved for people who died in climbing accidents.

name: Café de Paris
description: The birthplace of a famous steak sauce still keeps its recipe a secret.

name: Tower 5 of Felskinn Aerial Tramway
description: The only tower of an aerial tramway standing over a ski-lift.

name: Beetz Patented Urinal
description: One of the few places in the world that let's you legally relieve yourself on a UNESCO world heritage site.

name: Titeuf Statue
description: A statue of the Swiss cartoon character stands outside the school that inspired his creator.

name: Mosesbrunnen
description: This bunny-eared Moses represents an attempted compromise between two biblical interpretations.

name: Geneva Skateboard Musuem
description: One of the world's largest skateboard collections is on display inside a historical mansion.

name: The World’s Largest Ship Horn
description: On the water, the sound from this horn could be heard from up to 10 kilometers away.

name: Sodbrunnen Rennweg
description: A 700-year-old drinking fountain turned charity for the less fortunate of Zurich.

name: Guillotine of Geneva
description: During the French annexation, 36 prisoners were executed using this device.

name: Immeuble Clarté
description: An early masterpiece of Le Corbusier, a famed Modernist architect, hiding in plain sight.

name: Feuerwehrmuseum Basel
description: The world's largest collection of fire-fighting items dating back almost 350 years.

name: Elephant of Murten
description: The skeleton of a 19th-century elephant that was executed by cannonball.

name: Repère Pierre du Niton
description: This stone in Lake Geneva was once the reference point used to determine altitude in Switzerland.

name: Haus zum Goldenen Ochsen (House of the Golden Ox) Oriel
description: Explore the five senses depicted on this protruding Gothic window.

name: Liszt Memorial
description: A memorial to a 19th-century composer who was known for driving crowds wild.

name: John Calvin's Grave
description: A modest grave marks the final resting place of one of the world's most influential religious figures.

name: Charlemagne Statue in Grossmunster Crypts
description: A statue of Charlemagne, created in the 1400s, once overlooked the city, and is now located in a crypt.

name: Latenium
description: This museum is also an active archeological site at the eastern end of Lake Neuchâtel.

name: Hammering Man
description: This 42-foot tribute to the workforce still reminds us to pause and recharge.

name: Underground Mills
description: Water mills built into a cave in the 17th century are powered by a subterranean river.

name: Agrippa-d'Aubigné Terrace
description: This peaceful open space below Geneva's famous cathedral is full of art and history.

name: Villa 'Le Lac' Le Corbusier
description: The famed architect's minimalist home for his parents stands as a blueprint for his later architectural marvels.

name: London 2012 Olympic Truce Wall
description: The 21st century revival of one of the Olympic Games' oldest traditions, and its legacy.

name: Le Frigo
description: Walk through a restaurant's refrigerator door to reach this hidden bar.

name: Der Trauerautomat
description: An unusual vending machine designed to support mourners during funeral ceremonies at Zurich's largest cemetery.

name: Evolver
description: Spiral alpine structure provides an alternative experience of viewing the Matterhorn.

name: Juliertheatre
description: A tower-like theater in the Swiss Alps built to withstand the high winds and avalanches of the mountain pass.

name: Medizinhistorisches Museum
description: Featuring Zürich's only authentic 14th-century plague doctor's uniform!

name: Site of "The Absinthe Murders"
description: A tiny rural community in Switzerland was home to one of history's most shocking murders, and led to the banning of Absinthe for nearly a century.

name: 'Suitcase'
description: Though it may look like a businessman's lost luggage, this case suspended under a highway bridge is actually a contemporary art installation.

name: Blur Building
description: In memory of The Blur Building, the temporary exhibition pavilion of vaporous nothingness.
